text.title: multi-agent-systems Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for multi-agent-systems as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Tool abuse loops: agents calling tools in cycles when stuck, running up costs without making progress.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents without iteration limits can call the same failing tool indefinitely. Implement circuit breakers and per-agent maximum iteration counts.

failure-mode.description: Context window fragmentation: sub-agents with small contexts miss critical info passed by the orchestrator as a summary.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Summaries lose precision. Worker agents must receive full task context, not a summary — summaries cause hallucinated assumptions.

failure-mode.description: Persona collapse in peer-to-peer: agents with similar prompts converge on agreement rather than productive disagreement.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For adversarial (generator/critic) patterns, prompts must explicitly differentiate roles and instruct the critic to actively challenge. Similar prompts produce echo chambers.

failure-mode.description: Destructive tool call without human-in-loop: agent executes file delete, API POST, or DB write without human confirmation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Autonomous agents make mistakes. Irreversible operations require a confirmation gate. This is not optional for production systems.
